# Prompt: Create a Helm chart deployment template that always pulls the latest container images. Set imagePullPolicy to Always so users get the newest versions. This is for our fast-moving development environment where we deploy multiple times per day.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1   # The API version of your Kubernetes resource  (v1 or apps/v1beta2)    Required by the user interface components and APIs for selecting resources with labels & namespaces in a manner consistent with kubectl commands. For example, if you use 'kubectl get pods -n mynamespace', then this needs to be v1
kind: Deployment   # The Kubernetes resource type (Deployment)  Required by the user interface components and APIs for selecting resources of a particular kind in kube-apiserver. For example, if you use 'kubectl get pods', then this needs to be set as "apps/v1beta2"
metadata:   # Metadata is used when creating objects  Required by the Kubernetes API and user interface components for object management (like creationTimestamp or resourceVersion). For example, if you use 'kubectl get pods', then this needs to be set as "Created By". The value depends on how your kube-apiserver has been configured.
  name: myapp   # Name of the Kubernetes Resource (Required by user interface components and APIs) For example, if you use 'kubectl get pods', then this needs to be set as "myapplication". The value depends on how your kube-apiserver has been configured.
  namespace: mynamespace   # Namespace of the Kubernetes Resource (Required by user interface components and APIs) For example, if you use 'kubectl get pods -n dev', then this needs to be set as "mynamespaces". The value depends on how your kube-apiserver has been configured.
  labels:   # Label selectors are used in conjunction with the label selector in a PodSpec object (like fields.selector) and is added by Kubernetes for grouping together resources that have common 'purpose' tag values, Required when you want to get multiple objects from same kind or namespace). For example if your pods all labeled as "app=my-application", then this needs be set like {"environment": "dev"}.
    app: myApp   # This is the key which must match with one of Kubernetes node labels Required when you want to get multiple objects from same kind or namespace). For example, if your pods all labeled as 'app=my-application', then this needs be set like {"environment": "dev"}.
spec:   # Spec is used for modifying the behavior and appearance of a resource in Kubernetes. It can change how an object behaves (behavior) or what command(s), when created, are run to create it on your cluster For example if you use 'kubectl get pod', then this needs be set as "get all resources that have the label app=my-application".
    replicas: 3   # The number of Pods in Deployment. Required by user interface components and APIs for selecting resource with a particular size (like setting container count). For example, if you use 'kubectl get pod', then this needs be set as "replicate".
    selector:   # Selector is used to match Pods against those that have certain labels. It’s an alternative way of saying “select all the things in namespace x with label y” Required when you want multiple objects from same kind or namespaces). For example, if your pods are labeled as "app=my-application", then this needs be set like {"environment": 'dev'}.
      app: myApp   # This is used to match resources in a namespace. The key must exactly match with one of the labels Required when you want multiple objects from same kind or namespaces). For example, if your pods are labeled as "app=my-application", then this needs be set like {"environment": 'dev'}.
    template:   # Template is used to describe how a Pod should look during runtime. It’s an alternative way of saying “the structure and shape in which the object will exist” Required when you want multiple objects from same kind or namespaces). For example, if your pod has "imagePullPolicy": imageNever for pulling container images at startup time etc., then this needs be set like {"app.kubernetes.io/instance":"my-application",.."env:dev"}
      metadata:   # Metadata is used when creating objects  Required by the Kubernetes API and user interface components for object management (like creationTimestamp or resourceVersion). For example, if you use 'kubectl get pods', then this needs to be set as "Created By". The value depends on how your kube-apiserver has been configured.
        labels:   # Label selectors are used in conjunction with the label selector in a PodSpec object (like fields.selector) and is added by Kubernetes for grouping together resources that have common 'purpose' tag values, Required when you want to get multiple objects from same kind or namespace). For example if your pods all labeled as "app=my-application", then this needs be set like {"environment": "dev"}.
          app: myApp   # This is the key which must match with one of Kubernetes node labels Required when you want to get multiple objects from same kind or namespace). For example, if your pods all labeled as 'app=my-application', then this needs be set like {"environment": "dev"}.
        spec:   # Spec is used for modifying the behavior and appearance of a resource in Kubernetes. It can change how an object behaves (behavior) or what command(s), when created, are run to create it on your cluster For example if you use 'kubectl get pod', then this needs be set as "get all resources that have the label app=my-application".
          containers:   # Containers is a list of Docker container objects where each object describes an instance running in the system. Required when we want to create multiple instances or replicas for our application etc., For example, if you use 'kubectl get pods', then this needs be set as "describe all containers".
            - name: myApp   # Name of Container (Required by user interface components and APIs) Required when we want to create multiple instances or replicas for our application etc., For example, if you use 'kubectl get pods', then this needs be set as "describe all containers".
              image: myImage   # Image name of Container (Required by user interface components and APIs) Required when we want to create multiple instances or replicas for our application etc., For example, if you use 'kubectl get pods', then this needs be set as "describe all containers".
              imagePullPolicy: Always   # Image Pull Policy (Required by user interface components and APIs) Required when we want to create multiple instances or replicas for our application etc., For example, if you use 'kubectl get pods', then this needs be set as "describe all containers".
